parentQuestion: A marathon runner experiences severe muscle cramping and is found to have abnormally low sodium levels. Examination of their sports drink reveals no electrolytes, meaning they've been losing sodium through sweat without replacement. Which type of gland is responsible for the sodium loss?

scaffolds.explain: Sweat contains WATER AND ELECTROLYTES including SODIUM. Sweat isn't pure water — it contains sodium, chloride, and other minerals.

wrongExplains.0: Sweat contains dissolved electrolytes, not just water.
wrongExplains.2: Sweat has minimal protein; it's mostly water and salts.

options: Significant sodium and electrolytes are lost along with water
scaffolds.explain: Heavy sweating causes SIGNIFICANT SODIUM LOSS along with water. Marathon runners can lose 1-2 liters of sweat per hour, each containing sodium.

wrongExplains.0: Electrolytes are lost along with water.
wrongExplains.2: Sweating decreases sodium; it doesn't increase it.

scaffolds.q: How do sweat glands deliver sweat to the skin surface?
options: Through ducts to the skin surface
scaffolds.explain: Sweat glands deliver sweat to the SKIN SURFACE through DUCTS. This makes them EXOCRINE glands — they have ducts that deliver to a surface.

wrongExplains.0: Bloodstream delivery would make them endocrine.
wrongExplains.2: Ducts actively transport sweat to the surface.
wrongExplains.3: Nerves stimulate glands; they don't transport secretions.

options: It has ducts and delivers secretions to surfaces
scaffolds.explain: EXOCRINE glands have DUCTS and DELIVER TO SURFACES. 'Exo' = outside. They secrete to external or internal surfaces via ducts — like sweat glands.

wrongExplains.0: Secreting into blood defines endocrine glands.
wrongExplains.2: Hormones are made by endocrine glands.
wrongExplains.3: Exocrine glands have ducts; endocrine are ductless.

scaffolds.q: What type of secretion do sweat glands use?
options: Merocrine — cells release products by exocytosis and stay intact
scaffolds.explain: Sweat glands use MEROCRINE secretion — products are released by EXOCYTOSIS and the CELL STAYS INTACT. This allows continuous, sustainable secretion during a marathon!

wrongExplains.0: Holocrine (cell rupture) is used by sebaceous glands.
wrongExplains.2: Sweat glands keep cells fully intact, not losing apex.
wrongExplains.3: Endocrine describes gland type (ductless), not secretion mode.

scaffolds.q: Why can sweat glands continuously produce sweat during exercise?
options: New cells constantly replace dying ones
Merocrine secretion keeps cells alive and functioning
scaffolds.explain: MEROCRINE secretion keeps cells ALIVE and functioning. Cells survive and keep pumping out sweat — perfect for sustained sweating during exercise.

wrongExplains.0: In merocrine secretion, cells don't die.
wrongExplains.2: Sweat glands work continuously during exercise.
wrongExplains.3: Glands actively produce and secrete sweat.

scaffolds.q: Why aren't endocrine glands responsible for sweat and sodium loss?
options: Endocrine glands secrete into blood, not onto skin surfaces
Endocrine glands are the same as sweat glands
scaffolds.explain: ENDOCRINE glands secrete into BLOOD, not onto surfaces. Sweat goes onto skin (exocrine), not into bloodstream (endocrine). Different delivery routes entirely.

wrongExplains.2: Exocrine sweat glands make sweat, not endocrine.

scaffolds.q: What is the complete classification of sweat glands?
scaffolds.explain: Sweat glands are MEROCRINE EXOCRINE glands. EXOCRINE = has ducts to surface. MEROCRINE = secretion mode where cells stay intact.

wrongExplains.0: Sweat glands are merocrine (intact cells), not holocrine.
wrongExplains.2: Sweat glands are exocrine (has ducts), not endocrine.
wrongExplains.3: Sweat glands use merocrine, not holocrine secretion.

scaffolds.q: Which type of gland is responsible for sodium loss through sweat?
scaffolds.explain: MEROCRINE EXOCRINE glands (sweat glands) are responsible. Not holocrine (that's sebaceous glands), not endocrine (that's hormones into blood). Merocrine exocrine = sweat glands.

wrongExplains.0: Holocrine exocrine glands are sebaceous (oil) glands.
wrongExplains.2: Endocrine glands secrete hormones into blood.
wrongExplains.3: The eccrine (merocrine) sweat glands produce most sweat.
